en.extract:
The fire-crested alethe (Alethe castanea) is a species of bird in the Old World flycatcher family Muscicapidae. It is found in central Africa, from Nigeria to Uganda. Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests.
It has been recently split from the white-tailed alethe (A. diademata).

The '''fire-crested alethe''' ('''''Alethe castanea''''') is a species of bird in the Old World flycatcher family [[Muscicapidae]]. It is found in central Africa, from [[Nigeria]] to [[Uganda]]. Its natural [[habitat]] is subtropical or tropical moist lowland [[forest]]s.

It has been recently split from the [[white-tailed alethe]] (''A. diademata'').<ref name=ioc>{{cite web| editor1-last=Gill | editor1-first=Frank | editor2-last=Donsker | editor2-first=David | title=Chats, Old World flycatchers | work=World Bird List Version 6.2 | url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/bow/chats/ | publisher=International Ornithologists' Union| access-date=20 May 2016 }}</ref>
